title: Chapter Ninety-Seven: Landslide
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

It started subtly.

Vibrations beneath his feet. Different from the aftershocks of battle. A strange rumbling, rising from far away, layering and digging into the soles of their boots.

"Ah...?"

Yue Shou-lin's face changed to bewilderment.

The sound from above was unsettling and growing louder. In less than a breath, everyone present seemed to sense it. Crowds stretched along the ridgeline began to scatter, shouting.

"Mountain god -- the mountain god is angry!"

"Landslide! It's a landslide!"

Voices from those tied to the Namgung Family mixed in, but no one paid them heed. The word was right. Dust rose from several huge peaks flanking Clear Night Valley -- thick, massive, yellow-brown. Rocks and soil sliding together.

**RUMBLE.**

"Should we leave now?" Yue Shou-lin's dazed mumbling marked the start. Energy waves rose from the bodies of the Divine Sword Squad masters.

"This is all because the Group Leader had such a flashy fight with the Namgung Family Head..."

"Move to the rear! Take care of White Qilin first!"

Yeon-shin glanced sideways. Namgung Hwa-shin was being carried like a bundle by a senior member of the Divine Sword Group -- a towering man with a handsome, weathered face. The duel's end had been caused by nature itself. A disaster born of the clash between two supreme masters.

*With human bodies, they created a natural disaster.*

He ran. The senior smiled at him, then startled upon seeing Ma Yeon-jeok keeping pace beside the boy.

"What are you doing? Did you trade your martial energy for a slug's?"

Yue Shou-lin, running ahead, called back sharply. A spear strapped to her small back, she appeared the most composed among the Divine Sword Squad warriors.

But the Group Leader was the problem.

His movements were sluggish. For a man of his supremacy, it was difficult to believe. He seemed to be struggling to keep pace. His ki energy, once neat and orderly, was tangled like wet thread.

*The Group Leader's internal injuries are severe. The qi consumption -- I can't even estimate it.*

He recalled the moment after the Namgung Family Head's death. Yue Shou-lin casually telling the Group Leader to compose his breathing. In broad daylight, with countless martial artists watching -- what must the weight have been for the strongest warrior under the Desolate Fortress Lord?

*They must not show weakness to anyone.*

The boy understood it now. Showing vulnerability before the various sects -- the challenges Desolate Fortress would face from that single moment were unimaginable.

Several Divine Sword Squad members had already quietly surrounded the Group Leader. They knew. Of course they knew -- Desolate Fortress' premier fighting force could not fail to perceive their own leader's state.

"Hmm. That would be too conspicuous," the Group Leader muttered, not denying his condition. "Don't talk to me, all of you. It's difficult."

He forced himself onward while joking. His thick thighs moved his body with sheer physical strength. Despite his scholarly appearance, his physique was robust and highly trained. But the qi that should have supported him seemed absent.

**RUMBLE.**

"We need to get somewhere out of sight quickly," Yeon So-ha muttered, her voice strained.

"Faster! We don't know how far this will spread!"

Among the paths weaving through Clear Night Valley's ridges and ravines, numerous warriors were fleeing. Every one of them trained in lightness skills. Their sidelong glances toward the Desolate Fortress masters held an unsettling quality.

This was Namgung territory. How many of the fleeing martial artists harboured grudges against the lineage members who had died today?

"If we could just perform a single true qi circulation, it would get better..." Yue Shou-lin bit her lip.

"Only the Group Leader himself could manage that while using lightness skills," Yeon So-ha whispered.

"They're from the Martial Dragon Association. Coming down the ravine. If the Group Leader released one burst of energy, they'd turn back immediately."

"Blood Flame Sect and Deep Martial Alliance on the upper left. Those blood demons are never eradicated."

Four of the Thirteen Evil Sects. Closing in like wildcats.

"Grandfather."

"Speak, Yeon-shin."

Ma Yeon-jeok answered calmly. Despite the situation, he ran with the ease of an unorthodox immortal.

"Can't you use lightness skills while circulating true qi to calm internal injuries? You're a purple master."

"...It's difficult right now." The old man paused before speaking again. "Ma family martial arts are rough and fast. They build quickly and disperse just as swiftly. Maintaining consistency is difficult. It's easier to gain power in youth but hard to sustain."

"That's why you've been Group Leader since your green youth," Yue Shou-lin quipped, now closer.

Ma Yeon-jeok's eyes bulged. "Green youth? Yue family brat, back then you were a snot-nosed child yourself!"

"Must be nice, being old." Her gaze shifted. Through flowing hair, her eyes met Yeon-shin's. "Hey, kid. You've got a way, right? You've been rolling those sharp eyes around since earlier."

"...Yes."

"Come to think of it, your hand movements showed it -- your time perception was strange despite not entering the realm of ascension. Hurry! Do it quickly!"

Yeon-shin paused. "Having the Group Leader entrust his life gate to a martial artist he just met--"

"What do you mean?" The one who asked was the Group Leader himself. His breathing had grown visibly rough.

Warning voices rang from either side of the formation. Evil sects approaching. Blood Flame Sect. Deep Martial Alliance. Martial Dragon Association.

Yue Shou-lin pressed closer to Yeon-shin. "Lightning Genius. Is that blue robe and golden *Desolate* character just decoration? If you don't trust yourself, what can you trust? Group Leader -- say something. This kid's qi sense is extraordinary."

"Lightning Genius. This seat permits you to lay hands on my precious body."

Even in crisis, the man joked.

Yue Shou-lin grabbed Yeon-shin's hand and guided it to the Group Leader's back without hesitation. Her small hand -- returned to a young girl's form through reverse aging -- was unexpectedly soft.

*The calluses are shallow. Reverse aging isn't all good.*

He raised the Jeong family qi circulation technique.

*Old age... Right. First, I want to grow old.*

The boy wished for it. He needed to survive long enough to contemplate the topic Azure Qilin had left behind.

---
